cards.title: T2DM Reversal — Naturopathic Protocol
cards._legacyHtml:
Reversal definition: HbA1c <6.5% off all diabetes medications for ≥6 months (ADA 2023 criteria).
Core naturopathic interventions:
Diet: Low-carb (20–50 g/day) OR very low calorie (800 kcal/day × 8–12 weeks, DiRECT trial model). Remove all refined carbohydrates, sugar, liquid calories.
Fasting: Intermittent fasting 16:8 (daily) or 24-hr fast 2x/week. Reduces fasting insulin significantly.
Exercise: 30 min post-meal walk (↓ glucose spike 30%). Resistance training 3x/week (↑ GLUT-4 expression).
Key herbs: Gymnema sylvestre 400 mg BD (↓ HbA1c 1–2%), Berberine 500 mg TDS (comparable to metformin), Fenugreek 2 g/day, Bitter gourd juice 30 ml fasting, Cinnamon 2 g/day.
Hydrotherapy: Cold hip bath 20 min alternate days (↑ insulin sensitivity). Contrast foot bath daily.
Monitoring: FBS and PPBS weekly, HbA1c 3-monthly. Adjust medications with prescribing physician as glucose normalises.

cards.title: Metabolic Syndrome — Assessment & Management
cards._legacyHtml:
Diagnosis (IDF/AHA 2009 — any 3 of 5):
• Waist circumference: >90 cm (men Asian), >80 cm (women Asian)
• Triglycerides ≥150 mg/dL
• HDL <40 mg/dL (men), <50 mg/dL (women)
• BP ≥130/85 mmHg
• Fasting glucose ≥100 mg/dL
Root cause naturopathic view: Insulin resistance → hyperinsulinaemia → ectopic fat deposition → adipokine dysregulation → systemic inflammation.
Naturopathic management:
• Diet: Mediterranean + low-GI. Eliminate sugar, refined carbs, trans fats.
• Exercise: 150 min/week moderate aerobic + resistance 2x/week.
• Herbs: Guggulu (Commiphora mukul) for dyslipidaemia, Berberine for IR, Ashwagandha for cortisol/stress component.
• Hydrotherapy: Cold hip bath, Steam bath + cold shower (↑ thermogenesis).
• Target: 10% weight loss → significant improvement in all 5 parameters. Each 1 kg → ↓ systolic BP 1 mmHg.

cards.title: Hypothyroidism — Naturopathic Approach
cards._legacyHtml:
Assessment: TSH, Free T4, Free T3, Anti-TPO (Hashimoto's), Anti-Tg. Optimal TSH for symptoms: 0.5–2.0 mIU/L (not just 'normal range').
Root causes (address these): Iodine deficiency, selenium deficiency, autoimmune (Hashimoto's), heavy metal toxicity, gut dysbiosis, chronic stress (cortisol suppresses T3 conversion).
Nutritional support: Iodine — seaweed (kombu, nori), iodised salt. Selenium 200 mcg/day (↓ anti-TPO by 40% in Hashimoto's). Zinc 15 mg/day (T4→T3 conversion). Vitamin D 2000–4000 IU.
Herbs: Ashwagandha (KSM-66) 300 mg BD — ↑ T3/T4 levels, ↓ TSH (Sharma et al. 2018). Bladderwrack (iodine-rich, use cautiously). Avoid large amounts of raw cruciferous vegetables (goitrogenic) — cooking deactivates goitrogens.
Heliotherapy: Daily sun exposure (Vitamin D, thyroid function support).
Note: Never replace levothyroxine with herbs without physician. Herbs are adjunct only.

cards.title: Obesity Management — Naturopathic
cards._legacyHtml:
Assessment: BMI (Asian cutoff: overweight ≥23, obese ≥27.5), waist circumference, HOMA-IR, liver function, lipid profile, thyroid panel, fasting insulin.
Root cause framework: Hormonal (insulin, leptin, cortisol, thyroid), Gut microbiome (Firmicutes:Bacteroidetes ratio), Psychological (emotional eating), Environmental (obesogenic environment, endocrine disruptors).
Naturopathic protocol:
• Diet: Caloric deficit 500–750 kcal/day. Mediterranean / whole food plant-based. Eliminate UPFs (ultra-processed foods).
• Fasting: Intermittent fasting 16:8 or 5:2. Extended fasting under supervision (BMI >30).
• Hydrotherapy: Cold hip bath 20 min 5x/week (↑ adiponectin, ↑ thermogenesis). Steam bath + cold shower 2x/week.
• Yoga: Surya Namaskar 24 rounds, Kapalbhati 10 min, Agnisara daily.
• Herbs: Triphala 1 tsp at bedtime, Guggulu, Garcinia cambogia (caution: drug interactions), Green tea extract (EGCG 400 mg).
• Target: 0.5–1 kg/week safe rate. 5–10% loss → significant metabolic benefit.

cards.title: NAFLD — Non-Alcoholic Fatty Liver
cards._legacyHtml:
Diagnosis: Ultrasound (hyperechoic liver) + exclusion of alcohol. Confirm with FibroScan or liver biopsy if needed. AST:ALT ratio <1 in early NAFLD. Elevated GGT.
Staging: Simple steatosis → NASH (steatohepatitis) → Fibrosis → Cirrhosis. Early stages reversible.
Naturopathic management:
• Weight loss: 7–10% body weight ↓ = histological improvement (NASH). 500 kcal/day deficit.
• Diet: Mediterranean diet (best evidence for NAFLD). Eliminate fructose (HFCS), alcohol, refined carbs, red meat excess. Increase: coffee (2–3 cups/day — hepatoprotective), olive oil, oily fish.
• Herbs: Milk thistle (silymarin 140 mg TDS — ↓ ALT, ↓ AST, anti-fibrotic). Dandelion root. Turmeric (curcumin 500–1000 mg — ↓ hepatic fat).
• Exercise: Aerobic exercise ↓ hepatic fat independently of weight loss. 150 min/week minimum.
• Supplements: Vitamin E 800 IU/day (improves histology in non-diabetic NASH — AGA guideline). Omega-3 3–4 g/day. Choline-rich diet (eggs, liver).
• Monitoring: LFTs, ultrasound 6-monthly.

cards.title: Insulin Resistance — Assessment & Protocol
cards._legacyHtml:
Diagnosis: HOMA-IR = (Fasting Insulin × Fasting Glucose) / 405. Normal <1.0 (optimal). 1.0–1.9: borderline. ≥2.0: insulin resistant. ≥2.9: significant IR.
Clinical signs: Acanthosis nigricans, skin tags, central obesity, PCOS, fatigue after carbohydrate meals, sugar cravings, difficulty losing weight, high triglycerides with low HDL.
Root causes: Sedentary lifestyle, excess refined carbohydrates, chronic stress (cortisol), sleep deprivation, gut dysbiosis, Magnesium deficiency, toxin exposure.
Naturopathic reversal protocol:
• Diet: Low-carb or ketogenic initially. Eliminate sugar and refined carbs. Time-restricted eating 16:8.
• Exercise: Post-meal walks. Resistance training primary (↑ GLUT-4 translocation → glucose uptake without insulin).
• Key supplements: Magnesium glycinate 400 mg (co-factor for 300+ enzymatic reactions including insulin signalling). Berberine 500 mg TDS. Chromium picolinate 200–400 mcg. Inositol (D-chiro-inositol + myo-inositol 40:1) for PCOS-IR.
• Herbs: Gymnema, Fenugreek, Bitter melon. Monitor HOMA-IR at 3 months.
